ChIJmzWSur8EdkgRIoPuJsxp0pU.name: The Corner Café
ChIJmzWSur8EdkgRIoPuJsxp0pU.address: 179- UK, 182 Hercules Rd, London SE1 7JY, UK

reviews.text:
By the looks of most recent reviews, this place needs no introduction, but what’s another 5 star review eh?

Overwhelmingly deserving of the top honour, this very humble cafe serves delicious traditional breakfast, at honest prices, with huge portions. I was actually amazed when the plate hit my table.

The staff are lovely, and this cafe frequented by locals gives it a very grounded vibe. I’d love to claim that I have found a hidden gem, but it sits quietly in plain sight, noticed my many previous reviewers.

Cannot wait to return!

A very nice and friendly restaurant. The breakfast was delicious, everything was fresh and well prepared. The staff were welcoming and genuinely friendly, creating a pleasant atmosphere. Thank you for the tasty breakfast. We will definitely come back again.

The most quintessential English Breakfast I can imagine as an American tourist from NY. The care they give to every item is a joy to behold and to eat. What to get? Everything you can fit into your gullet.

ChIJKzfar20OdkgRC_aFViPKR54.name: London Buddhist Vihara
ChIJKzfar20OdkgRC_aFViPKR54.address: The Avenue, Chiswick, London W4 1UD, UK

reviews.text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Europe’s Oldest Theravada Buddhist Temple – A Truly Peaceful Spiritual Home

London Buddhist Vihara is known as the oldest Theravada Buddhist temple in Europe, and it truly feels like a sacred and comforting spiritual home. I deeply love this temple because it brings peace, calmness, and healing to the mind the moment you enter.

The Chief monk ( Ven.Bogoda Seelavimala )is incredibly kind, compassionate, and deeply inspiring, offering wisdom, guidance, and warmth to everyone who visits. The vihara is beautifully maintained, spiritually uplifting, and filled with positive energy, making it a perfect place for meditation, prayer, and reflection.

Whether you are Buddhist or simply someone seeking peace, calmness, and clarity in life, I warmly invite everyone to visit this wonderful place. You will truly feel blessed to be here. 🙏❤️

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I sent my wife there to check out this for the first time. She met with a monk. They had a good chat and she was asked whether she could buy some yarn of a certain colour from Singapore. And she did.

Quite easy to locate.
Originally built by Anagarika Dharmapala, it was the first ever Buddhist temple built on British soil. Housed inside a house it has that calming, tranquil feeling.

ChIJ_UZdjtsEdkgRcHnYGRr8sw0.name: St James's Café
ChIJ_UZdjtsEdkgRcHnYGRr8sw0.address: Horse Guards Rd, London SW1A 2BJ, UK

reviews.text: St. James’s Cafe in St. James’s Park is located in a beautiful area overlooking St. James’s Park Lake just step away from sites like Big Ben and Westminster Abbey. There’s plenty to choose from here for food and drink, including pre-packaged sandwiches, fresh made items from the deli kitchen and the sit-down menu. If you choose to sit at a table, order via QR code from there. When I was here it was pretty busy but my food arrived in a timely fashion. My Breakfast Bap and glass of wine were delicious. Seating is available outside during warmer weather. Very much recommended.

Overall I had a very nice experience here. The staff member who took my order was friendly and the hot chocolate was delicious.

While I briefly stepped away to use the restroom, my drink was cleared from the table. I had left my belongings there, but since there wasn’t much of the drink left, it may have been an understandable misunderstanding. It was just a little disappointing as I had hoped to relax and finish it.

Aside from that, everything else was great.

We visited St James's Café during a weekday lunch. Despite being busy, the café’s spacious layout made it easy to find a seat.

Orders were placed at the counter, and I chose the fish and chips paired with a hot chocolate. The food arrived promptly. The fish and chips were fresh and flavourful, while the hot chocolate was pleasantly balanced, not overly sweet, and suited my palate perfectly. Overall, it was a very satisfying meal.

The bankside café is the greatest place we’ve been to in London. Good food with great staff ! We paid 18£ for two full English breakfast and the plate were huuuuuuge. Thought that we had to add black pudding but the waitress told me that it was included ! Recommand this place more than others

The distance of this cafe was not very far from where we were staying. The food i got to say was brilliant. The cafe serves and caters to a lot more than just breakfast as you can have lunch and dinner here.

The staff welcomed you in which is always a nice touch they dont pressure you at all when placing orders. I have no complaints about this place the value for money was great.

The place was clean and tidy and i would 100% recommend this place.

Came to get the kids some non-sourdough toast, ended up ordering a cheese and ham roll which was lovely. Big lumps of cheese and nice ham. Delicious. The breakfasts being served were massive and plenty of people around enjoying them. Would return for good value food. Not high end but good honest grub nicely priced.

London Waterloo Station is one of the busiest and most important transport hubs in the UK, and it generally leaves a strong impression on both regular commuters and first-time visitors. The station is almost always busy, especially during peak hours, but it remains well organised considering the sheer volume of passengers passing through each day. Despite the crowds, movement through the concourse is usually smooth, with clear signage that makes it easy to find platforms, exits, and connections to the Underground or nearby attractions. One of the station’s biggest strengths is the constant presence of railway employees. Staff members are visible throughout the station and are usually approachable, helpful, and knowledgeable. Whether you need directions, platform information, or assistance with tickets, there always seems to be someone nearby ready to help and guide you in the right direction, which is reassuring in such a large and busy environment. Cleanliness is another positive aspect of London Waterloo Station. For a station that handles such heavy footfall every day, it is consistently clean and tidy. Platforms, walkways, and waiting areas are well maintained, and bins are regularly emptied, helping the station feel orderly rather than overwhelming.Overall, London Waterloo Station is a reliable, efficient, and well managed transport hub. While it can feel crowded at times, the helpful staff, cleanliness, and good organisation make it a generally positive experience for passengers.

Busy station that is not easy to navigate but not terribly difficult either, especially with the help of the friendly staff. As long as you position yourself in front of one of the main screens by the gates to the platforms you get informed in plenty of time to make it to your train along with the car you want. TIP: The graffiti tunnels are a great 10-minute detour if you arrive early and need to kill time.

Amazing architecture and the biggest Terminus station in London. Plenty of shops including Boots, TG Jones, M&S. Plenty of eateries on the concourse. Staff are always friendly and very helpful. The station is a five minute walk to the South Bank by the Thames. Many bus routes, a Sainsbury's and a lovely fish and chips shop called
The Fishcotheque. Situated just under the railway bridge just outside. IMAX Cinema is also situated just outside the station. Beautiful Station. Toilets are always clean and well looked after. Fully disabled access here too.

A cozy place, an hidden gem within the heart of East Acton. I keep revisiting this place for a hearty meal at least twice every week. It's become a favourite spot! The food is top notch! Creamy hot chocolate and wholesome pizzas.

We grabbed a meat feast pizza from this spot and it honestly hit the mark—tasty, loads of toppings, proper flavour. But the thing that stood out the most was the owner. The guy was genuinely nice, super chilled, and just had good energy. Out of nowhere he gave us free Cheez-It “just for fun,” which actually made the whole visit feel even better. Little moments like that make a place memorable. Good pizza, good people. Would happily go back.

Lovely spot and does just what you want from it. It's unpretentious, cheap and cheerful with friendly staff in an accessible location. Kid friendly too!

Pizzas are excellent quality for the price, have had them to eat in and take away as well. My daughter loves the Margherita pizzas, I tend to go for the pepperoni options, all very good quality at pocket friendly prices. Really recommend to any locals looking to support small independent businesses!

One of the best breakfasts I have ever eaten. The full English was excellent, elevated to a higher level than most with the use of seasonings, garlic and garnish. Every item in the packed skillet was of a high quality and cooked perfectly, I particularly loved the home made herby sausage patty. Pork belly on a breakfast was something I've never seen before but was juicy and succulent. Place was very clean and staff were friendly and efficient,   the owner even coming out to chat to everyone was a nice touch. If you want a top class breakfast that is above and beyond the average breakfast (and a great pot of tea) you must try this cafe ! Highly recommend. 10*

Fam went on a week vacation in London and found this gem, went here twice, met the amazing owner and enjoyed authentic english breakfast 😋

All dishes we tried were cooked fresh and tasted excellent 👌

The owner made sure everyone is taken cared of. These kind of small cafés are hard to come by these days.

Highly recommend to check it out if you are visiting London area. 🙌👍

On to the next! 🫡

came here during the last day of our london adventure, met the lovely owner who made us DELICIOUS food, i even recieved a complimentary piece of white pudding because the owner wanted me to try it, so delicious. Paolo engaged in some lovely conversation about our travels and gave us tips for our upcoming days, such a lovely cosy place. would recommend some more coffee training but overall we would 100% return 🩷

The Almeida is one of the best producing theatres in London. Some of their shows go to the West End and Broadway.

They champion new work as well as bringing fresh perspectives to revivals.

The space is very flexible and can be a proscenium, of thrust, or even in the round, whatever is best for that particular play.

They don't shy away from controversial plays and musicals, so this is the place to go if you want to be challenged as well as entertained.

Tip: When you book seats downstairs it may say restricted view. That's because this is an old venue with poles holding up the balcony. As you can see in the picture there small and in my experience they don't really get in the way. If that bothers you the seats upstairs are excellent.

It was afternoon of performances from drama students doing extracts of theatre performed over the years at the almeida. Really high standard as I expected. And only £12 for 3.5 hours of theatre, pretty good. Some context about what was going on would be useful but still very entertaining.

First visit to the Almedia Theatre.
Front of House staff. Friendly and helpful.
Cafe/bar reasonable
I think the theatre is across  buildings. But the performance space in one.

Show: Portia Coughlan
A moving and difficult at times play. The cast is excellent. Especially, Alison Oliver as the titular Portia. The play set in Ireland through I've not met an Irish woman called Portia. Kathy Kiera Clarke is excellent in both comedic parts and the more serious elements of the play.
A very powerful play.

Second visit, 1536, set during the fall of Queen Anne Boleyn it charts a brief period of time in the lives of three Essex girls. Whilst socially by class divided from Anne. By sex their freedom to act as we understand that freedom.
The cast of two male actors supporting powerful performances from all three of the female leads.
The last act and conclusion comes as a shock. Hopefully a transfer to the a west End will follow. A must see.
Front of house and cafe/bar staff very good.

Third visit: A Moon for the Misbegotten, set in 1923 rural Connecticut tells the tale of an Irish immigrant farmer (David Threfellan) and his daughter Josie (Ruth Wilson) as they try to tame the land after his last son leaves  superficially about plots and intrigue, the play to me is about being authentic to self.
An excellent cast, studio dressing and direction.
I attended the relaxed performance which was interesting.

Theatre Tour, regular tours throughout the year. Excellent history, and view of behind scenes areas, and a step onto the stage (particular stage for Christmas Day). The guide Nick very knowledgeable and engaging. Definitely, worth doing.

Fourth show. Christmas Day. A Jewish father visits his children and partner at their unorthodox living space for Christmas Day. And weaves it way with Jewish humour and references as to how Jews mark Christmas. The play 1hr 50mins is a powerful piece.
The play as presented and written are different. The book contains an additional scene with a hitherto unseen flatmate returns (which was explained in the being as everyone else 12 we're at home). The deletion of the scene does not weaken the final play.

Sunday afternoon - boyyy the wait was long… but SO worth it!
Gentleman at the door handled everything with such a controlled and calm demeanour.
Staff inside were very nice and pleasant.
BUT THE FOOD! The food hit the spot! Perfectly seasoned and flavours were so good! Im not usually this impressed.
The food came out quick too. Would highly recommend getting the halloumi fries.
Coffees were also spot on!
My first proper brunch since moving to London and I’m so glad it was here. Well done to the owners and management.

Will definitely be returning InshaAllah

⸻

This is honestly one of the best breakfasts I’ve ever had. We’re regulars here, and because of that we’ve bumped into the owner a few times — him and my partner are now acquaintances. When we come in and he’s there, he always comes over to us personally, which just adds to how welcoming the place feels.

I usually go for the Wild West breakfast with the Milieu signature beans, and those beans are incredible. I have genuinely never had beans this good anywhere else — they’re rich, flavourful, and elevate the whole breakfast.

The hash browns are perfectly crispy on the outside and soft and fluffy on the inside. The pancakes are delicious — light, fluffy, and served with fresh strawberries and berries every time.

I’ve also tried the mac and cheese bites, and they’re a nice option if you love cheese. Definitely worth trying.

For drinks, I usually get an iced caramel latte, which I always enjoy — it’s consistently great.

The customer service is also amazing! The staff are so friendly and attentive.

Overall, I’d rate this place a 10 out of 10. There’s nothing to fault; everything is perfection. If you’re looking for a new breakfast spot, this is definitely the place to check out.

Incredible food, staff are welcoming & friendly. Customers need to be mindful of the wait time & are required to join a minimum 1hr queue over the weekends after 10am regardless of group size.

Overall, great time & atmosphere within.

All our dishes were the chef’s signature.

Thank you to Yunis & his team on Sunday, 1st Feb 2026.

ChIJteLYIO8cdkgRxYGV9AfNElI.name: London Fields Lido
ChIJteLYIO8cdkgRxYGV9AfNElI.address: London Fields West Side, London E8 3EU, UK

reviews.text: Amazing 50m outdoor pool open year round. Very well maintained and clean locker rooms. Lanes can get very busy and chaotic in the summer with novice swimmers, but it’s worth it for the outdoor space.

A great local, heated lido - well maintained, good and friendly staff and a mix of regulars, locals and passers through. It feels part of the local landscape and part of the local community.

Personally I love a winter's swim when the steam is rising and the air is crisp and the sky a pure blue - but it's great at anytime of the year.

It is unsurprisingly very busy on a hot summers day - but still swimmable if you come with patience and a forgiving attitude.

This place is lovely, first of all it being outdoors is a really nice change, you don’t get too hot and it’s great to be outside.
The pool is always immaculately clean, quite impressive there’s never any leaves etc in there, water is really clear. Always warm enough, great changing facilities and plenty of them. Staff are really amazing, so friendly and great instructors too. I just love this place, hope nothing ever changes!

ChIJ3QxXoloABDQRlw86bPG_6EI.name: Queen Elizabeth Stadium
ChIJ3QxXoloABDQRlw86bPG_6EI.address: 18號 Oi Kwan Rd, Wan Chai, Hong Kong

reviews.text: On 24 May 2025, my wife and I came here to watch Nasatoshi Nakamura’s concert in Hong Kong. Whilst he is just a few years older than me, the star and singer-songwriter personified a TV drama hero that I adored in the mid-1970s. It was a full house and the audience were mostly senior citizens. The Stadium’s location and facilities are user-friendly.

the setting of sound is good here and it’s a good place for holding a small concert here, first time watching a Cappella and it’s fun haha and people’s voice is so powerful the music can be so complicate even without music instruments haaa

rating of mine: 4

Beautiful Stadium/arena with plenty of space for spectators and center stage floor for competitions of any kind. The lighting equipment is very nice and professional resulting in great photographies taken. Changing rooms are also quite spacious and comfortable with mirrors and lights for better makeup sessions.

Such a lovely spot at Shoreditch! ☕✨ I really enjoyed my time here. The coffee was delicious and perfectly made, and the breakfast was fresh, tasty, and very satisfying. You can tell they really care about quality.
I also loved the concept and overall vibe of the place — it feels cozy and thoughtfully designed. Even the menu design is super stylish and adds to the whole experience.
Definitely a place I’d come back to and highly recommend!

I came to this place with a friend at around lunchtime on a Saturday. The place is not big, rather cosy, and most tables were occupied during the time we were there, but not crazy crowded. My friend had a savoury breakfast and I had sweet pancakes with fruit and ice-cream. It's not super fancy food, but everything looked great with decent portions. I really enjoyed my pancakes and would definitely go back to have them again. They were soft and almost crispy outside, and the combination with the fruit, ice cream and syrup made it very tasty. We also had a coffee and it was a great coffee for my taste, on the sweet side rather than bitter or acidic. We paid around 20 pound each, which is pretty good for London and we liked everything. I would recommend and go back to this place.

My favourite breakfast spot in Shoreditch. The main dishes also looked great, but since I went in the morning, I had the chance to try their breakfast — absolutely worth it.

If you’re ever around Old Street, I’d definitely recommend stopping by. I enjoyed a lovely breakfast while soaking in the warm atmosphere inside and the view outside. I had the halloumi, avocado and roasted vegetables on sourdough, and it was absolutely delicious, highly recommend.

I also really liked the overall vibe of the café and the background music — it all added to the experience. The staff were attentive and the service was excellent, thank you! I’ll definitely be back.

We had breakfast here on the first day of our London trip and absolutely loved it! Naturally, we had to come back a few days later. I highly recommend the Eggs Benedict, Çılbır, and the Turkish breakfast—they were all delicious. The staff were incredibly kind. Thank you for everything! 🌸💕

The food was so good! The staff are so lovely ! There were some halal options, you just need to ask the staff. We had the Mediterranean breakfast and the morning turkey breakfast. Love that you have options of sweet and savoury breakfast! And it comes with a good variety. We just walked around and found this cute little place close to Islington for lunch. The food is also decently price - cheap for a central London brunch spot and it actually tastes good and fills you up. Found our new fav little hideaway cafe ! They have the cutest decor around!

Absolutely amazing! The breakfast was outstanding – every little detail was carefully thought out. The quality, flavor, and presentation were simply perfect, and the prices are unbeatable.
I’ve traveled a lot around the world, but I’ve never had such a delicious and lovingly prepared breakfast for this kind of price.
A special shout-out to the waitress, who was incredibly kind, attentive, and genuinely sweet – especially with children. She treats them with such warmth and even gives them a lovely little surprise that makes the experience even more special.
I can highly recommend this place – it’s the perfect way to start your day!

We had our Wedding breakfast and celebrations in Fidelio and it was the best decision we made for our wedding without a doubt. I’m so so happy I found Fidelio!

Ash is an incredible Manager and made every effort to help make our wedding vision come to life.

On the day everything went perfectly and the food was absolutely delicious. Our family and friends loved Fidelio and we’ve had so many people tell us that they had a great evening there.

Photos by Nicola Drummond Photography
The best bit about choosing Fidelio for your wedding/party is that you’ll be able to go back and relive it by just visiting the cafe!

Thank you Ash & team for giving us the best wedding day we could have ever imagined!!!

I recently hosted my leaving doo at Fidelio and it couldn’t have gone better.

The manger, Ash, was fantastic from start to finish. He completely understood the vibe I was hoping for and went above and beyond to make it special for my guests and me. Nothing was too much trouble. Everything felt easy, relaxed and genuinely well looked after.

The food was great, the space is beautiful, cosy and unique. The whole evening just flowed. It felt intimate without being formal, and special without being overdone.

I would 100% recommend this venue for a event or party.

Lovely cafe that I stumbled across on a recent work trip. The scrambled eggs were fantastic with a great atmosphere. Relaxing jazzy playlist and suitable for working there with no rush to leave. Good coffee too!
One of my favorite high schools I attended
WONDERFUL SCHOOL = THE BEST EDUCATION IN LONDON!
Wrong school in the wrong place. No respect for the fact it was a school in Britain, or the culture around it.

Hyde Park is one of the largest and most famous parks in London. It is located in the center of the city and is a popular place for relaxation and outdoor activities. People visit Hyde Park to walk, ride bicycles, have picnics, or enjoy nature. The park is also known for Speakers’ Corner, where people can freely express their opinions. Hyde Park is an important green space and a favorite destination for both locals and tourists.

Hyde Park is a great place to relax in the middle of London. It’s spacious, well maintained, and perfect for walking, sitting by the lake, or just taking a break from the busy city. I enjoyed the calm atmosphere, open green spaces, and the mix of locals and tourists. A must-visit if you want some fresh air and peace in London.

Hyde Park is such a relaxing and beautiful place in the middle of London. It’s perfect for walking, chilling, or just taking a break from the busy city.
The park is huge, green, and peaceful, and it’s great in any season. Whether you want to walk around, sit by the water, or enjoy some fresh air, it’s always a good idea

I visited this location a few times while in Richmond. It's very cozy and the staff are very nice. You can dine inside or outside at the many tables. The toilets are clean, just ask the staff for the code for the door. One of the best cups of coffee I had while in the UK.

Great cafe tucked under the Richmond Bridge with a beautiful scenic view of the river and the old three. Had a nice flat white and a spinach feta pastry which was delicious. Excellent vegetarian options both sweet and savoury. Plenty of outdoor seating tables for reading with the coffee or just simply people watching by the river.
Nice cafe by the river under the bridge. It's small inside but it has plenty tables outside if warm enough to sit outside.

ChIJL7zxE_IMdkgRCtW5GSlFJCU.name: World Rugby Museum & Allianz Stadium Tours
ChIJL7zxE_IMdkgRCtW5GSlFJCU.address: Allianz Stadium, 200 Whitton Rd, Twickenham TW2 7BA, UK

reviews.text: What a great stadium tour, so much history to see as well as a true modern rugby stadium. Iconic does not come close.

Great experience at the home of England Rugby. The tour of the stadium was comprehensive and the guide was very knowledgeable. For any sports fan this is a must do. Short walk from Twickenham Station.
Brilliant. Very interesting. The tour was fantastic and our guide, Mike, very pleasant and knowledgeable

ChIJJzYMYAAPdkgRedNXVqRKW1A.name: Moo cafe Restaurant persian
ChIJJzYMYAAPdkgRedNXVqRKW1A.address: 42 Fulham Palace Rd, London W6 9PH, UK

reviews.text:
This is a local hidden gem. Had such a wonderful experience. The staff were extremely friendly and the owner made the experience seem so personal.
The food was delicious and great  coffee! The atmosphere is peaceful and serene, providing a lovely escape from the hustle and bustle. Highly recomended.

I absolutely loved this cafe! The coffee was rich and flavorful, and the cakes were freshly made and so delicious.
The staff were incredibly kind and welcoming  they made me feel at home.
The atmosphere was cozy and relaxing with such a positive vibe
Such a wonderful little gem - the owners are so friendly, cinnamon buns are delicious and the best part is the hospitality. Feels like visiting a friend’s home - thank you for the smiles! ♥️

ChIJtSERLYkOdkgRSGdUwBMSD1k.name: Euro Cafe (Full English Breakfast, Pancakes, Coffee & Juices)
ChIJtSERLYkOdkgRSGdUwBMSD1k.address: Dry Cleaners, 45 Sheen Ln, London SW14 8AB, UK

reviews.text:
Popped in here for a coffee early last week.

Had a really warm welcome in this early opening cafe.

Situated very near the train station, it had a little charm in the decor with a selection of vintage wireless sets in the wall.

Coffee was good.

Had a television with the news on so one can start the day well informed.

Food did look ok as I glanced at the ither diners and there were many others there.

This would be a good place to check out if you are in the area and need a bite if pick me up.

The breakfast is so good here. The coffee is great and the bread they used for my toast was really light and toasted perfectly. Highly recommend this place. The staff are also lovely. Great place very chilled and really good value for money

I am so pleased to find just a normal cafe! Prices are not inflated to some 'boutique' cafes, and the food is top-notch. I haven't been able to find a good old bubble and squeak on a menu for a long time! Oh, and it was spot-on, as was the bacon, eggs cooked to my taste, tomatoes, and sausage. A lovely couple running the place, too. My breakfast set me up for the rest of my day! Even managed to park, literally, right outside for free - another rarity. Thank you.

Incredible! We had heard good things about this place. There was a bit of a queue but we decider to wait it out. Certainly glad we did! The queue went down really quick and the food was incredible!! All 4 of us have said how nice the food was and we all had different dishes. A lot of choice and wish we could visit more often to try it all. 5/5!!

This was probably the most enjoyable place I visited in England, both in terms of the time I spent and the food I had. When I learned that the owner is Turkish, I wasn’t surprised at all that the place is this good and well run. The hospitality and genuine care we received were truly delightful.

The salmon was the best I’ve ever had, incredibly tender and buttery. We were told it’s smoked using traditional methods, and the quality really shows. It’s a busy spot, and for good reason. Once you go, you immediately want to come back.

I will definitely visit again next time I’m in the area. If I’m remembering the names correctly, special thanks to Çınar and Şahin. It was a pleasure meeting you.

Had breakfast at Drury Covent Garden this morning and honestly it was a lot better than I expected for such a quick stop. I wasn’t looking for anything big, just something light to start the day, so I went with a croissant, another coffee, and a grilled egg. Sounds simple, but the whole thing actually came together really nicely.

The place itself has a relaxed vibe, kind of that “London café but not trying too hard” feel. People were just doing their own thing — some working, some chatting, some half-asleep like me — and it didn’t feel chaotic or rushed at all. The staff were chilled too, not overly chatty but friendly in that normal, human way. They took my order, kept it moving, and didn’t make it awkward.

The croissant was warm and soft on the inside with just enough flakiness on the outside, but not the kind that makes crumbs fly everywhere. The grilled egg was actually really good, cooked properly and tasted fresh, not rubbery or dry like you sometimes get. And the coffee… honestly, that was the best part. Strong enough to wake me up but still smooth. Not bitter, not burnt, just solid.

I sat there for a little while and the whole breakfast just felt easy and enjoyable. Nothing fancy, nothing dramatic, just a decent morning meal that hit exactly right. I’d happily swing by Drury Covent Garden again for another quick breakfast like that.

ChIJO1vhVbAEdkgRPoJKQ9gTOh4.address: London Nautical School, 61 Stamford St, London SE1 9NA, UK

ChIJD7FsstMadkgRbjUI2TpkHAs.name: The London Welbeck Hospital
ChIJD7FsstMadkgRbjUI2TpkHAs.address: The London Welbeck Hospital, 27 Welbeck St, London W1G 8EN, UK

reviews.text:
If i could rate The Wellbeck with 10 stars, I would. I feel all staff have gone above and beyond and made me feel instantly at ease, safe and cared for.
The reason I am compelled to write this review is a very special nurse called Esther. Her clinical skills have been outstanding, from explaining safety / reasons why I had been prescribed pain medication to why I needed to complete certain health checks that otherwise I might have not prioritised. Her levels of care are the best I’ve ever received. Esther is a truly outstanding nurse, despite me stating I was “ fine” when under the influence of medication, she knew the signs that indicated in wasn’t and acted calmly but safely and got me to my bed before I tainted. I could continue, I don’t have enough words to give this incredible lady justice.

There are some other people that also massively impacted my patient experience, Julie the anaesthetist, her skills and warmth absolutely helped me to feel informed, safe and cared for, from discussing lifestyle factors that could influence my health outcomes to explaining what would happen on the way in to the operating room. Pain management- excellent too.

Ida, who took care of me through my admission, helping me settle in, she was calm, reassuring, she explained after care beautifully and most importantly made me feel like she was caring for me like a family member would. She demonstrated integrity, she didn’t answer my questions with vagueness or any generic detail, she answered with honesty and transparency.

There was a HCPN, I forgot her name, i briefly met her but she was also wonderful, caring and had a fantastic bedside manner.
She reassured me I was in safe hands.

Finally Mr Marsh, simply incredible, his clinical skills speak for themselves, however, he really took me by surprise with his warmth, caring and attention to detail. He listened to me, his care was extremely attentive and individual to me. He’s hands down the most amazing consultant I have met.

A truly outstanding and wonderful experience throughout a time of vulnerability and life changing surgery.

Thank you to each and every one you, I am very grateful for this amazing experience.

I had the most amazing experience at the Wellbeck Hospital as a day patient.Every member of staff i had dealings with, so professional and caring. Special thank you to my nurse Ida what a lovely lady and so interesting to talk too.
Also the lad on reception who helped me into my Uber.

I would highly recommend this hospital its a pearl in the middle of the west end.

Keep up the good work.

Ann from epsom

My wife attended the Welbeck hospital on 12.12.25 for breast augmentation. Receptionist wasn't very friendly when greeting just passed clipboard like fill this out, could of be more welcoming considering its private care. The sugeon Dr Lahoud was brilliant as always and the surgery room team. Facilities clean and comfortable. ##### The London Buddhist Vihara

The London Buddhist Vihara is a leading centre for Theravada Buddhism. Founded in 1926 by Anagarika Dharmapala, the Vihara was the first Buddhist monastery to be established outside the continent of Asia. It has continued its task of disseminating the Dhamma with resident bhikkhus (monks) from Sri Lanka throughout this period, with the exception of the 1940s due to World War II. The Vihara moved to Chiswick during 1964 when the Anagarika Dharmapala Trust (ADT) of Sri Lanka purchased the freehold property at 5, Heathfield Gardens. In 1994 the Vihara moved to new spacious premises in The Avenue, Chiswick, London W4. In 2003, Vihara Management Committee (VMC) was formed to manage LBV acted as attorneys for ADT. ## Welcome to Tide Tables

![Image 1](http://www.tidetablescafe.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/2018/06/welcome-image-1.jpg)

##### Not to be missed Tide Tables is the place to hang out all day and watch the world go by. Richmond is as little as 15 minutes from Waterloo by train, connected to the rest of London by underground and bus routes, this famous waterside cafe is marvelously situated right on the Thames at Richmond with one of the prettiest views of the river in the whole of London.

##### We are two sister cafes on the banks of the Thames at Richmond. One café in a converted arch beneath Richmond Bridge. The other café is in the middle of the famous flower gardens at Terrace Gardens. A great place if you’re alone or with friends. The food is strictly vegetarian, and there are vegan and gluten-free menus.Fairtrade and organic products are available, and we only use organic milk. We are licensed and serve organic wines, beers and ciders. We don’t charge extra for cow milk alternatives.

##### From January 2022 onwards we shall be making an extra charge for cow’s milk in our coffees to help keep plant milk low-priced for everyone.

## Welcome to Drury - Est. 2016

Welcome to Drury 188-189, recognised as the best cafe in London and Covent Garden on TripAdvisor thanks to our exceptional coffee, delicious brunch, and cost and inviting atmosphere. Located in the heart of Covent Garden, we offer a Wi-Fi-free space to disconnect from the madness of the city. Whether you’re here for a quick drink or a relaxing breakfast with a friend, our unique menu and lovely staff make Drury 188-189 the go-to spot for locals and visitors alike. Swing by and discover why we’re celebrated as being the top cafe in London today.

No booking needed. Walk-ins only.

## Help keep Drury the best cafe in London and Covent Garden

## We want to hear from you! Have your say. Tell us about your experience after visiting us and help others to find us!

## Made with passion.

## Our Menu

At Drury 188-189, and its sister cafe Drury N4, we offer unique menus that have been developed by our talented chefs. Whether you’re enjoying looking for a healthy and nutritious lunch, or a sweet treat to indulge in – our menu has something for everyone.

To support our local community, our menu is crafted with care, using locally sourced ingredients to support nearby businesses. Each of our locations in London brings something special to the table.

Please select the menu for your preferred location below to discover what we have in store for you at one of the best cafes in London.

![Image 4: Best rated cafe in London and Covent Garden](blob:http://localhost/4e9b9e9935b9e0de213166d242eef8bc)

![Image 5: Outside of Drury 188-189](blob:http://localhost/beedf9aeccbca8768fc087be944b73ef)

## Breakfast, Brunch & Lunch

## Rated Best Cafe in London and Covent Garden

Find us in two iconic London spots—Covent Garden, home to the world-famous Drury 188-189, and Woodberry Down, home to our sister cafe Drury N4. Although part of the same brand, each location offers its unique menu and atmosphere. Check out our locations section below to plan your visit and view our opening hours here. We look forward to welcoming you to one of London’s best cafes, whether you’re visiting us in the heart of the city or venturing out to our modern concept in Woodberry Down.

## About us

## Two Best Friends On A Journey

Drury 188-189 began in 2016 as the passion project of two best friends from East London – Ali and Cemal who quit their 9-5 jobs in pursuit of creating a brand new concept in the independent cafe scene in London. Tired of corporate life, they wanted to create a cafe that had no wi-fi and encouraged people to relax with a great book or simply keep each other company and create new friendships. Eight years on, Drury is now one of the most beloved cafes in the city which reflects their commitment and serves as a testament of what can be achieved when you have an amazing team and a vision.Today, Drury 188-189 is the highest-rated cafe in London thanks to our amazing customers and their kind reviews throughout the years.

## OUR STORY

Welcome to joon: a family-run, independent cafe driven by a love of culinary exploration and speciality coffee. Since April 2025, we’ve been honoured to proudly call the Warren Street neighbourhood home.

You may notice our menu is delightfully multicultural...so are we. We find joy in the union of global flavours and culinary traditions; we aim to offer a slice of home, wherever home may be. At our core is a belief that the true art of hospitality has the power to brighten every day.

In Farsi, ‘joon’ means life and soul. It can be used as a term of affection: ‘joonam’ means my life, my soul. For us, the love and dreams cemented in the walls here, and every customer that becomes family, is ‘our joonam’
